feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: American tests positive for Ebola; U.S. to screen travelers at airports
entry.summary: Health experts are growing increasingly concerned about the outbreak, given that cases could have been spreading undetected for weeks.

entry.title: Argentina’s icy outpost at the end of the world fears the hantavirus will chill tourism
entry.summary: Remote Ushuaia in Argentina, known as the southernmost city on earth, has found itself at the center of a global media storm involving the deadly hantavirus outbreak.

entry.title: Parkinson’s Drug Restores Memories in Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: Restoring dopamine levels with Levodopa reverses memory decline in Alzheimer's disease models.

entry.title: Trump and RFK Jr. Boosted This Unproven Autism Drug. Some Parents Listened
entry.summary: The FDA recently declined to endorse leucovorin for autism, citing a lack of strong evidence for it.

entry.title: How safe are GLP-1 drugs, really?
entry.summary: GLP-1 medications have exploded in popularity over the past few years, and it’s easy to see why. They work. People lose significant weight, blood sugar improves, and for the first time in a long time, weight management feels achievable. But anytime a drug goe…

entry.title: People Are Getting A Little Freaked Out After Learning These 31 Wild Facts About The Human Body
entry.summary: "Memories and songs are stored in two different parts of the brain, so people with Alzheimer’s won’t remember their family, but they’ll remember songs."

entry.title: Scientists Found a Remarkable Way to Help Kids Like Vegetables
entry.summary: Trying to persuade kids to eat healthily is a challenge many parents face, and a small new study suggests that pregnancy might be a prime time to influence toddlers' tastes.

entry.title: Chicago Is 2nd Worst City For Mosquitoes. How Bad Will This Summer Be?
entry.summary: Orkin Pest Control ranked Chicago No. 2 in the United States in mosquito activity over the past year, though what that means for this summer is “up in the air," some experts said.

entry.title: The grain debate: Are refined carbohydrates really bad for the heart?
entry.summary: Are refined grains truly bad for your heart, or are they simply guilty by association? A look at the research reveals a more nuanced picture, writes Carrie Dennett.

entry.title: These are the U.S. states monitoring for hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship at center of outbreak
entry.summary: Officials in Arizona, California, Georgia, Kansas, Maryland, Minnesota, New Jersey, Texas, Virginia and Washington say they are monitoring residents after...

entry.title: metabolic fitness and immune homeostasis in the gut
entry.summary: This study describes that cardiolipin deficiency-induced mitochondrial dysfunction in Treg cells impairs gut–immune tolerance.

entry.title: Column | Head and neck cancers are on the rise. Here’s how to reduce your risk.
entry.summary: A few major factors are driving new cases of head and neck cancers. Luckily, they are things you can control.

entry.title: Where You Live May Affect How Rapidly You Age, Sweeping Study Finds
entry.summary: The agents guiding the shape of your aging journey may not just come from within.

entry.title: Make Sure You’re Eating These Foods By Your 40s If You Want To Live Longer
entry.summary: A handful of key dietary habits could significantly increase your lifespan.

entry.title: People On GLP-1s Say They Suddenly Have This 1 Unexpected Obsession
entry.summary: As food becomes less stimulating, GLP-1 users are gravitating toward this pricey obsession.

entry.title: Vermont rejected the federal government’s vaccine rollbacks. But doctors are still seeing their impact.
entry.summary: The latest wave of vaccine skepticism puts an added load on an already-strained primary care workforce, clinicians say.

entry.title: Common Cleaning Chemical Could Triple Your Risk of a Dangerous Liver Disease
entry.summary: Scientists are uncovering a possible connection between everyday chemical exposure and serious liver damage.

entry.title: At least six Americans exposed to Ebola in DR Congo, US media report
entry.summary: One of the six Americans believed to have been exposed is experiencing symptoms, according to media reports.

entry.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands
entry.summary: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak​​ has docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put world health authorities on alert.

entry.title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection
entry.summary: A cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection. The MV Hondius reached Rotterdam on Monday morning, carrying 25 crew members and two medical personnel. All passengers had disembarked already. The c…

entry.title: Five minutes of exercise a day could help millions of people live longer
entry.summary: Small increases in physical activity as part of our daily lives can bring long-term benefits to our health.

entry.title: A Danish Couple’s Maverick African Research Finds Its Moment in RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policy
entry.summary: The work of Peter Aaby and Christine Stabell Benn has long been controversial. Until Robert F. Kennedy Jr. became US health policy chief, most vaccine scientists tended to ignore it. Now they can’t.

entry.title: The 'fibermaxxing' trend has health benefits worth the hype
entry.summary: There are a lot of wellness trends that make health experts roll their eyes in skepticism. But there is one big trend that many experts can get behind – with a few caveats: fibermaxxing.

entry.title: Argentina’s icy outpost at the end of the world fears the hantavirus will chill tourism
entry.summary: Remote Ushuaia in Argentina, known as the southernmost city on earth, has found itself at the center of a global media storm involving the deadly hantavirus outbreak. Local tourism operators and officials are scrambling to stem the reputational fallout that t…

entry.title: Americans may have been exposed in Congo Ebola outbreak
entry.summary: The WHO declared the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern.

entry.title: 'We mould trees to grow into the shape of chairs'
entry.summary: A couple from Derbyshire have spent the past 20 years testing designs for homegrown chairs.

entry.title: The 6 High-Fiber Ingredients Dietitians Recommend Adding to Your Salads
entry.summary: Trying to hit your fiber goals for the day? Dietitians recommend adding these toppings to your salad for a fiber boost.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Over 1,700 'Dark' Proteins Hidden in Human Cells
entry.summary: A new study has revealed a previously hidden layer of the human genome.

entry.title: Fifth measles case
entry.summary: ID'd in Los Angeles County this year

entry.title: There is a moment near death, documented in EEG recordings of dying patients, when the brain produces a coordinated burst of gamma wave activity more intense than anything measured in waking life — and no one knows what it is, what it's for, or what the
entry.summary: In the final minutes of life, the dying brain has been observed producing a synchronized surge of high-frequency electrical activity that exceeds anything recorded in healthy, waking humans — and the scientific literature still cannot say what it means.

entry.title: Humans Age Faster at 2 Sharp Peaks, Research Shows
entry.summary: Getting older might seem like a slow, gradual process – but research suggests that this is not always the case.

entry.title: Scientists identify several different ways that ADHD can manifest in people, along with three different brain profiles
entry.summary: Brain scans revealed a hidden and more severe form of ADHD in children, characterized by significant emotional disturbances.

entry.title: The Best Way to Eat Sweet Potatoes for Maximum Vitamin A Absorption, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Dietitians reveal the best way to prepare and serve sweet potatoes to maximize the amount of vitamin A absorption in the body.

entry.title: In Ebola outbreak, a number of Americans in the Congo believed to have had exposure to suspected cases
entry.summary: A number of Americans who are in the Congo are believed to have had exposure to suspected cases in the country’s latest Ebola outbreak, sources have told STAT.

entry.title: New Study Challenges What We Know About Consciousness and the Brain
entry.summary: A new study reveals that the brain may continue interpreting language and predicting information even while unconscious under anesthesia.

entry.title: Greek Yogurt vs. Cottage Cheese—Which Protein-Packed Snack Comes Out on Top?
entry.summary: Discover the better high-protein snack between Greek yogurt and cottage cheese. Here, nutrition experts reveal which dairy product comes out on top, and why.

entry.title: Polymarket Spreads Sensationalist Garbage About Hantavirus Case in U.S.
entry.summary: They're certainly not helping prediction markets with credibility.

entry.title: Fish oil might actually be bad for you–so what’s a better alternative?
entry.summary: An April study from the Medical University of South Carolina found that fish oil could actually do more harm than good.

entry.title: "My Boss Kept Commenting on My Body": Why My Fake 'Weight Loss Journey' Made Him Threaten to Cry.
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Douglas County adult dies from hantavirus; case not linked to cruise ship outbreak
entry.summary: A Douglas County adult resident has died from hantavirus, state and county health officials reported on Saturday.

entry.title: Canadian national health agency confirms 1 positive hantavirus test
entry.summary: Canada's national health agency Sunday confirmed that one of four Canadians who returned home from a cruise ship hit with a hantavirus outbreak has tested...

entry.title: Canadian national health agency confirms 1 positive hantavirus test
entry.summary: Canada's national health agency confirms that one of four Canadians returning from a cruise ship with a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive. The Public Health Agency of Canada announced the positive test Sunday, following initial reports from the public h…

entry.title: Tick bites surge, sending many to ER
entry.summary: Several regions are seeing increased tick activity due to warmer temperatures, especially in the Northeast and Midwest, according to the CDC.

entry.title: I Normally Have a Much Higher Sex Drive Than My Husband. Then He Got Injured, and, Uh, Wow.
entry.summary: This is very unusual for me.

entry.title: Mediterranean Diet May Activate Secret Anti-Aging Proteins Inside Your Cells
entry.summary: The findings highlight a new frontier in “precision nutrition,” where biomarkers could help personalize diets to support better health and longer life.

entry.title: ‘Don’t kiss, snuggle’ poultry: 4 sick in Georgia from multi-state salmonella outbreak
entry.summary: Four people in Georgia are sick from a salmonella outbreak that’s already confirmed to have made almost 200 people ill.

entry.title: Scientists Reveal That Eating Almonds Every Day Could Transform Your Gut, Metabolism, and Appetite
entry.summary: A new feeding study suggests that replacing common processed snacks with a daily serving of almonds may do more than improve nutrition.

entry.title: Omaha Steaks, Nerf basketball and Hazmat suits: Former contagion patients describe life in quarantine amid Hantavirus
entry.summary: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the nation’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are s…

entry.title: ‘We’re in for a bad year’: Scientists see warning signs for Lyme disease, tick-borne illnesses
entry.summary: As the weather warms up, scientists have a dire warning before you head outdoors.

entry.title: Scientists uncover surprising health benefits of watermelon
entry.summary: Studies suggest watermelon could be a hidden powerhouse for better health. Researchers found that people who eat watermelon tend to have higher-quality diets packed with more vitamins, fiber, and antioxidants — while consuming less added sugar and saturated f…
